title: Listening - Part 1 - Exercise 7
description: You are going to hear people talking in eight situations. For questions 1–8, choose the correct answer (A, B or C). Mark your answers on the separate answer sheet.
instructions: You will hear each piece twice. For questions 1–8, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You hear a message from a choir leader.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Hi Olivia, it’s Mr James from the school choir. Just a reminder that rehearsal is at four tomorrow in the music hall. We’ll practise the same songs for next month’s concert, so bring your folder as usual. ||Man: The school photographer is coming at the start, and [1]everyone must wear a plain black top for the group photo[/1]. It will only take a few minutes.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Hi Olivia, it’s Mr James from the school choir. Just a reminder that rehearsal is at four tomorrow in the music hall. We’ll practise the same songs for next month’s concert, so bring your folder as usual.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: The school photographer is coming at the start, and everyone must wear a plain black top for the group photo. It will only take a few minutes.

questions.question: What does Mr James remind Olivia to do?
questions.explanation: The choir leader says everyone must wear a plain black top for the group photograph (evidence [1]).

extracts.context: You hear two friends talking about a cooking class.
extracts.audio_script: Girl: How was the cooking class you started last night? Were the recipes complicated? ||Boy: Not at all. The instructor explained everything clearly, and my vegetable soup was better than I expected. ||Girl: Are you going again next week? ||Boy: Yes, though [2]most of the course seems to be about making cakes, and I’d rather learn more everyday meals[/2]. I’ll suggest it to the teacher.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: How was the cooking class you started last night? Were the recipes complicated?

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Not at all. The instructor explained everything clearly, and my vegetable soup was better than I expected.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Are you going again next week?

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Yes, though most of the course seems to be about making cakes, and I’d rather learn more everyday meals. I’ll suggest it to the teacher.

questions.question: What would the boy prefer the course to include?
questions.explanation: The boy would prefer to learn more everyday meals rather than mainly making cakes (evidence [2]).

extracts.context: You hear an announcement at a railway station.
extracts.audio_script: Announcer: Passengers waiting for the 13.05 service to London should be aware that the train will arrive at Platform Four in approximately ten minutes. The café carriage will be open as normal. ||Announcer: However, because of a problem with the air conditioning, [3]the quiet coach is closed today, and anyone holding a reserved seat there may sit in any unreserved place[/3]. We apologise for this inconvenience.

dialogue.speaker: announcer
dialogue.text: Passengers waiting for the 13.05 service to London should be aware that the train will arrive at Platform Four in approximately ten minutes. The café carriage will be open as normal.

dialogue.speaker: announcer
dialogue.text: However, because of a problem with the air conditioning, the quiet coach is closed today, and anyone holding a reserved seat there may sit in any unreserved place. We apologise for this inconvenience.

questions.question: What are passengers told about the quiet coach?

options.A: It is unavailable on today’s train.
options.B: It has moved to the front of the train.
options.C: It can only be used by people with reservations.

questions.explanation: The announcement says the quiet coach is closed because of an air-conditioning problem (evidence [3]).

extracts.context: You hear a radio interview with a wedding photographer.
extracts.audio_script: Presenter: Is travelling to beautiful places the best part of being a wedding photographer, Laura? ||Woman: It can be enjoyable, although carrying equipment on trains isn’t always fun. I also like choosing the best pictures afterwards. But [4]what I enjoy most is meeting couples and families and making them comfortable in front of the camera[/4]. That’s the part requiring real skill.

dialogue.speaker: presenter
dialogue.text: Is travelling to beautiful places the best part of being a wedding photographer, Laura?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It can be enjoyable, although carrying equipment on trains isn’t always fun. I also like choosing the best pictures afterwards. But what I enjoy most is meeting couples and families and making them comfortable in front of the camera. That’s the part requiring real skill.

questions.question: What does Laura most enjoy about her work?
questions.explanation: Laura says her favourite part is meeting people and helping them feel comfortable in front of the camera (evidence [4]).

extracts.context: You hear a customer ordering food in a café.
extracts.audio_script: Waiter: Would you like anything else after your sandwich? We have chocolate cake and fresh fruit salad today. ||Woman: I was going to have the chocolate cake, but I’ve just seen a sign saying it’s sold out. ||Waiter: I’m sorry. We do have apple pie. ||Woman: I don’t really want pastry. [5]I’ll have the fruit salad instead, please[/5], and a cup of tea.

dialogue.speaker: waiter
dialogue.text: Would you like anything else after your sandwich? We have chocolate cake and fresh fruit salad today.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I was going to have the chocolate cake, but I’ve just seen a sign saying it’s sold out.

dialogue.speaker: waiter
dialogue.text: I’m sorry. We do have apple pie.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I don’t really want pastry. I’ll have the fruit salad instead, please, and a cup of tea.

questions.question: What dessert does the woman order?
questions.explanation: Since the chocolate cake is unavailable and she does not want pastry, the woman chooses fruit salad (evidence [5]).

extracts.context: You hear a teacher talking to a pupil about a school trip.
extracts.audio_script: Teacher: Daniel, I haven’t received your permission form for Saturday’s trip to the castle. We needed all the forms yesterday. ||Boy: I left it in my bag. Can I bring it tomorrow? ||Teacher: I’m afraid [6]all the coach seats have now been given to pupils who returned their forms on time[/6]. I can put your name on the waiting list in case someone cancels. ||Boy: Oh, I see.

dialogue.speaker: teacher
dialogue.text: Daniel, I haven’t received your permission form for Saturday’s trip to the castle. We needed all the forms yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: I left it in my bag. Can I bring it tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: teacher
dialogue.text: I’m afraid all the coach seats have now been given to pupils who returned their forms on time. I can put your name on the waiting list in case someone cancels.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Oh, I see.

questions.question: Why might Daniel be unable to go on the school trip?

options.A: He cannot find the permission form.
options.B: There may no longer be space on the coach.
options.C: The trip has been moved to another day.

questions.explanation: The teacher says all coach seats have already been allocated to pupils who returned forms on time (evidence [6]).

extracts.context: You hear two friends talking about a furniture delivery.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: The new sofa is being delivered tomorrow morning. I’m glad we found one in the sale. ||Man: Yes, but it looked quite wide in the shop. ||Woman: That’s what worries me. [7]Could you measure the front doorway tonight, before the delivery van arrives[/7]? If it’s too narrow, I’ll phone the shop and ask about another way in. ||Man: Good idea. I’ll find the tape measure.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: The new sofa is being delivered tomorrow morning. I’m glad we found one in the sale.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, but it looked quite wide in the shop.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That’s what worries me. Could you measure the front doorway tonight, before the delivery van arrives? If it’s too narrow, I’ll phone the shop and ask about another way in.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Good idea. I’ll find the tape measure.

questions.question: What does the woman ask the man to do?

options.C: check the width of the entrance

questions.explanation: The woman asks him to measure the doorway before the sofa is delivered (evidence [7]).

extracts.context: You hear a radio presenter talking about a community garden.
extracts.audio_script: Presenter: The Green Corner community garden will be open to visitors this Saturday from ten until four. Volunteers will show beginners how to grow herbs, and tea and homemade biscuits will be sold to raise funds for new tools. ||Presenter: [8]The garden also has extra tomato and strawberry plants, which visitors may take home without paying while supplies last[/8]. Bring a bag if you would like one.

dialogue.speaker: presenter
dialogue.text: The Green Corner community garden will be open to visitors this Saturday from ten until four. Volunteers will show beginners how to grow herbs, and tea and homemade biscuits will be sold to raise funds for new tools.

dialogue.speaker: presenter
dialogue.text: The garden also has extra tomato and strawberry plants, which visitors may take home without paying while supplies last. Bring a bag if you would like one.

questions.question: What can visitors receive free at the community garden?
questions.explanation: Visitors may take home extra tomato and strawberry plants at no charge while supplies last (evidence [8]).
